title: CRS 001 — Watson Two Routes
subtitle: Route A: IBM generate() · Route B: LangChain invoke & pipes — two bites only

nodes.label: set_env.ps1
nodes.definition: Loads WATSONX_MODEL_ID, URL, PROJECT_ID, APIKEY into the shell — once per session before any helper import.
nodes.whyItMatters: Both routes read the same four env vars. No env → KeyError or model is None.
nodes.safeSentence: Run set_env.ps1 first; both IBM and LangChain routes use WATSONX_*.
nodes.commonTrap: Importing watson_helper or watson_llm before env is loaded.
nodes.interviewAnswer: Credentials live in environment variables loaded by a setup script; helpers read them at import.

nodes.label: Route A — IBM
nodes.definition: Low-level watsonx: ModelInference, model.generate(str), dict out — you slice generated_text.
nodes.whyItMatters: Coursera LOCAL SETUP, Lab 15, raw API shape — not LangChain.
nodes.commonTrap: Putting model in prompt | model | parser.
nodes.interviewAnswer: IBM SDK path uses ModelInference.generate and returns vendor JSON; not a Runnable.

note.summary:
Remember ONE file:
  from watson_helper import model

NOT watson_llm for Route A.

nodes.label: watson_helper
nodes.definition: Playground plug-in that exports model (ModelInference) built from WATSONX_* env.
nodes.whyItMatters: Single import for IBM path in .py labs — no credentials in script.
nodes.safeSentence: watson_helper.model is ModelInference — IBM door only.
nodes.interviewAnswer: watson_helper wraps ModelInference for direct generate() calls.

note.summary:
from watson_helper import model, model_id, credentials

msg = model.generate("Hello")
text = msg["results"][0]["generated_text"]

nodes.label: model.generate()
nodes.definition: IBM text generation call — string prompt in, full response dict out. Not invoke().
nodes.whyItMatters: Different method name and return type than LangChain — mixing them breaks pipes.
nodes.safeSentence: generate() returns a dict; extract generated_text yourself.
nodes.commonTrap: Calling llm.invoke on ModelInference or expecting a plain str back.
nodes.interviewAnswer: ModelInference uses generate(); response is structured JSON, not a LangChain string.

note.summary:
msg = model.generate("In today's sales meeting, we ")
print(msg["results"][0]["generated_text"])

nodes.label: Route B — LangChain
nodes.definition: WatsonxLLM via make_watsonx_llm(): invoke(str) or prompt | llm | parser — str out.
nodes.whyItMatters: All LCEL labs, chains, agents — everything composable uses this route.
nodes.safeSentence: Route B = make_watsonx_llm() → invoke or pipes.
nodes.interviewAnswer: LangChain wraps watsonx as WatsonxLLM Runnable for invoke and LCEL composition.

note.summary:
Remember ONE file for Route B:
  from watson_llm import make_watsonx_llm

Notebooks + labs share the same two files (PYTHONPATH).

nodes.label: watson_llm
nodes.definition: Canonical LangChain builder: make_watsonx_llm(), make_watsonx_chat(), one-shot llm_model().
nodes.whyItMatters: Thick helper — the only LangChain file you need in memory.
nodes.safeSentence: watson_llm builds WatsonxLLM for invoke and LCEL.
nodes.commonTrap: Thinking watson_llm is low-level IBM (name sounds like watson_helper).
nodes.interviewAnswer: watson_llm is the LangChain factory; watson_helper is the IBM SDK factory.

note.summary:
from watson_llm import make_watsonx_llm, llm_model, GenParams

llm = make_watsonx_llm()
llm.invoke("Capital of France?")

# one-shot:
llm_model("Capital of France?")

nodes.label: llm.invoke()
nodes.definition: LangChain call on WatsonxLLM — plain string in, string out (text API).
nodes.whyItMatters: Standalone tests and simple scripts without building a full pipe.
nodes.safeSentence: invoke() on WatsonxLLM — not generate() on ModelInference.
nodes.commonTrap: Using generate() on the LangChain object.
nodes.interviewAnswer: LangChain LLMs expose invoke for Runnable-compatible calls.

note.summary:
llm = make_watsonx_llm()
print(llm.invoke("Who is man's best friend?"))

nodes.label: prompt | llm | parser
nodes.definition: LCEL pipe — WatsonxLLM must be step 2; StrOutputParser or JsonOutputParser last.
nodes.safeSentence: Only WatsonxLLM in the pipe — never watson_helper.model.
nodes.interviewAnswer: LCEL requires Runnable steps; WatsonxLLM is Runnable, ModelInference is not.

note.summary:
chain = prompt | make_watsonx_llm() | StrOutputParser()
chain.invoke({"content": passage})

nodes.label: Trap: model in pipe
nodes.definition: Using watson_helper.model after | in LCEL — ModelInference is not a Runnable.
nodes.whyItMatters: Most common wiring mistake when both routes share the same env.
nodes.safeSentence: Pipes need make_watsonx_llm(); generate path needs watson_helper alone.
nodes.commonTrap: Same WATSONX_* env so you assume any imported model works in chains.
nodes.interviewAnswer: IBM SDK and LangChain wrappers are different types despite shared credentials.

note.summary:
❌ prompt | model | parser   (model from watson_helper)
✅ prompt | make_watsonx_llm() | StrOutputParser()

nodes.label: 2 bites only
nodes.definition: Memory budget: generate()? → watson_helper. Else → watson_llm.make_watsonx_llm().
nodes.whyItMatters: Project uses two files only — notebooks import same helpers as .py labs.
nodes.safeSentence: Two routes, two files — watson_helper and watson_llm.
nodes.commonTrap: Memorizing six helper filenames instead of two decisions.
nodes.interviewAnswer: I pick IBM SDK for raw generate or LangChain WatsonxLLM for invoke and composition.

note.summary:
A) watson_helper  →  model.generate()
B) watson_llm       →  make_watsonx_llm() / invoke / |

Env: set_env.ps1 (both)

paths.label: Route A lane
paths.description: IBM SDK: env → watson_helper → generate → slice text.

paths.label: Route B lane
paths.description: LangChain: env → watson_llm → invoke or pipe.
